---
title: "glasso_path"
generated: true
description: "Warm-started graphical-LASSO regularisation path over `lambdas`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.connectivity`](../nitrix-stats-connectivity.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:glasso_path -->
```python
glasso_path(S: Float[Array, 'p p'], lambdas: Float[Array, 'L'], *, n_outer: int = 100, n_inner: int = 50) -> Float[Array, 'L p p']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:glasso_path -->
Warm-started graphical-LASSO regularisation path over `lambdas`.

Sweeps the penalties in the given order, carrying the working covariance
`W` and the lasso coefficients `B` from one penalty to the next so that
each solve starts warm.  This is far cheaper than a cold restart at every
penalty and is the usual way to trace a graphical-LASSO path; either sweep
direction (descending, dense to sparse, or ascending) warm-starts.  Pair the
returned precisions with `ebic_score` for model selection.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:glasso_path -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `S` | `Float` | `p` × `p` | — | Sample covariance matrix shared across all penalties. |
| `lambdas` | `Float` | `L` | — | The `L` non-negative L1 penalties, swept in the given order. |
| `n_outer` | `int` | — | `100` | Number of outer column sweeps per solve. Default `100`. |
| `n_inner` | `int` | — | `50` | Number of inner lasso sweeps per column. Default `50`. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `L` × `p` × `p`. The stacked precision matrices, one `(p, p)` estimate per penalty and aligned with `lambdas`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:glasso_path -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `L` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
